EMPLOYERS ARE EXPECTED TO HIRE ONLY ABOUT 790,000 TEENAGERS DURING THE MAY–JULY PERIOD, THE LOWEST LEVEL SINCE RECORDS BEGAN.

HIGHER INFLATION, ELEVATED FUEL COSTS, AND A BROAD SLOWDOWN IN HIRING HAVE REDUCED SEASONAL HIRING BY RETAILERS, RESTAURANTS, CAMPS, AND OTHER TRADITIONAL EMPLOYERS OF TEENS.
